questions.question: What effect size did the Mediterranean diet show for reducing depressive symptoms?

options.text: No effect at all
Moderate effect (SMD = -0.53) with significant reduction in symptoms
Only effective for severe depression
The Mediterranean diet increases depression

questions.explanation: The Mediterranean Diet meta-analysis (2024) showed a moderate effect size (SMD = -0.53) with significant reduction in depressive symptoms. Benefits were seen within weeks of dietary changes. This supports nutrition as a meaningful component of depression management.

questions.question: What is the relationship between sleep and depression?

options.text: Sleep has no connection to depression
Bidirectional - poor sleep increases depression risk, and depression disrupts sleep; 80%+ of depressed people have insomnia
Only depression affects sleep, not the reverse
Sleeping more always helps depression

questions.explanation: Sleep and depression have a bidirectional relationship: poor sleep increases depression risk (insomnia doubles it), while depression disrupts sleep. Over 80% of people with depression experience insomnia. Poor sleep maintains depression even when other factors improve.

questions.question: Why should alcohol NOT be used as a sleep aid or coping mechanism for depression?

options.text: Alcohol is expensive
Alcohol is a CNS depressant that provides temporary relief but worsens depression long-term, disrupts sleep architecture, and can interfere with treatment
Alcohol has no effect on depression
Alcohol only affects sleep, not mood

questions.explanation: Alcohol is a central nervous system depressant that may provide temporary relief but worsens depression long-term. It disrupts sleep architecture (even if it helps you fall asleep), can interfere with medication effectiveness, and using it to cope is a warning sign.

questions.question: What are the four domains of sleep hygiene?

options.text: Only the bedroom environment matters
Schedule/timing, pre-sleep routine, sleep environment, and substances/activities
Pillows, mattress, blankets, and sheets
Sleep hygiene has no defined domains

questions.explanation: The four domains of sleep hygiene are: (1) Schedule and timing (consistent bed/wake times), (2) Pre-sleep routine (wind-down activities, dim lights), (3) Sleep environment (dark, cool, quiet), and (4) Substances and activities (caffeine, alcohol, screens, exercise timing).

questions.question: What is the 'Worry Time' technique for managing racing thoughts at night?

options.text: Worry as much as possible right before bed
Schedule 15 minutes earlier in the day to write out worries, so when they arise at night you can remind yourself they've been addressed
Never think about worries at all
Set an alarm to wake up and worry

questions.explanation: The Worry Time technique involves scheduling 15 minutes earlier in the day to write out worries. When worries arise at night, you remind yourself: 'I've already addressed that during worry time.' This contains worry to a specific time rather than letting it invade sleep.

title: Food, Sleep, and Lifestyle Factors - Quiz
